name: Refrigerator No-Cheese Cake
recipeIngredient: Sugar substitute equivalent to 1 tablespoon sugar
Sugar substitute equivalent to 1 tablespoon sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush graham crackers in a plastic bag and tie the top. Use a rolling pin or jar to make fine crumbs.
2. Melt margarine in the bottom of an 8- or 9-inch round cake pan.
3. Spread margarine evenly over the bottom of the pan.
4. Sprinkle crumbs evenly on the bottom only and press gently.
5. Place the pan in the refrigerator and chill until set.
6. Soak gelatin in cold water.
7. Dissolve gelatin by heating it over boiling water.
8. In a large bowl, combine yogurt and first measure of sweetener. Beat with rotary beaters at moderate speed, adding dissolved gelatin gradually.
9. Chill mixture until it reaches the consistency of beaten egg whites.
10. In another bowl, mix iced water, lemon rind, egg white, and dry milk powder. Beat until soft peaks form.
11. Add lemon juice, vanilla, and second measure of sweetener. Beat at high speed until stiff.
12. Fold the stiff mixture into the partially set yogurt and blend well.
13. Spoon the mixture over the crumbs in the pan and chill for 4 hours or longer until set.
14. To unmold, loosen around the edge of the mold with a thin spatula down to the bottom of the pan.
15. Place a larger plate upside down on top of the mold.
16. Turn the plate and pan over; cover the top of the pan with a hot cloth that has been run under hot water and then wrung out.
17. Remove the cloth and lift the pan from the mold.
18. Garnish with cherries or strawberries and cut into six equal slices.
6 Servings | 1 Serving: 1/6 Cake
Nutritive values per serving: 13 grams carbohydrates; 8 grams protein; 2 grams fat; 104 calories; 0.3 gram fiber; 124 milligrams sodium; 6 milligrams cholesterol
Food Exchange per serving: 1 Low-Fat Milk Exchange
Low-sodium diets: This recipe is suitable.
